2603.18945.title: A conceptual framework for ideology beyond the left and right
2603.18945.paper_summary: NLP+CSS work has operationalized ideology almost exclusively on a left/right partisan axis. This approach obscures the fact that people hold interpretations of many different complex and more specific ideologies on issues like race, climate, and gender. We introduce a framework that understands ideology as an attributed, multi-level socio-cognitive concept network, and explains how ideology manifests in discourse in relation to other relevant social processes like framing. We demonstrate how this framework can clarifies overlaps between existing NLP tasks (e.g. stance detection and natural language inference) and also how it reveals new research directions. Our work provides a unique and important bridge between computational methods and ideology theory, enabling richer analysis of social discourse in a way that benefits both fields.
2603.18945.paper_summary_zh: NLP+CSS 的工作幾乎完全在左/右的黨派軸上實現了意識形態。這種方法掩蓋了人們對於種族、氣候和性別等問題持有許多不同複雜且更具體的意識形態解釋的事實。我們介紹了一個將意識形態理解為一個屬性化的、多層次的社會認知概念網絡的框架，並解釋了意識形態如何在話語中與其他相關社會過程（如框架）相互關聯。我們展示了這個框架如何澄清現有 NLP 任務（例如立場檢測和自然語言推理）之間的重疊，以及它如何揭示新的研究方向。我們的工作提供了一個獨特且重要的橋樑，連接計算方法和意識形態理論，使得社會話語的分析更加豐富，並使兩個領域都受益。
2603.18945.author: Kenneth Joseph et.al.
2603.18945.authors: Kenneth Joseph, Kim Williams, David Lazer
